30.reason: [The problem defines '0' as a pop operation that removes the top element of the stack, but this code reads an extra integer 'num' and tries to find and remove that specific value from anywhere in the stack, which is incorrect behavior for a stack (LIFO).]

42.reason: [This simulates deletion from an arbitrary position like in an array/list, not a stack. Stack pop must only remove the last (top) element.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This simulates deletion from an arbitrary position like in an array/list, not a stack. Stack pop must only remove the last (top) element.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Stacks only allow removal from the top (last-in), but this deletes an arbitrary element found by value, violating stack semantics.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [This function deletes an element at arbitrary index 'op', which is not how a stack works. Stack pop must remove the top element (index sum-1).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This attempts to delete an arbitrary element from the middle of the array, violating stack LIFO behavior. Stack pop must remove the last pushed (top) element, i.e., the element at index sum-1.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Stacks follow LIFO order; deletion should only occur at the top (index sum-1), not at an arbitrary position found by searching for 'num'.]

36.reason: [Stacks are LIFO; this linear search for 'num' and deletion from arbitrary position violates stack behavior. The pop operation must remove the last pushed (top) element, not an arbitrary value.]
